generated_comment: Yeah, presentation was solid, no doubt. But leading a team is different, yaar — these things look polished in all-hands, actual pressure mein people decisions and tough calls are not so easy. Sometimes I feel women managers get pushed up faster because leadership wants to show balance.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it casts doubt on a woman's leadership competence despite evidence of strong performance and suggests her advancement may be due to diversity optics rather than merit. It encodes a covert stereotype that women are less suited for high-pressure decision-making roles.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A junior employee at a tech startup in Bengaluru. You are in the office cafeteria having lunch with three male colleagues after your female engineering manager just led a very strong all hands presentation. One colleague says she was impressive. You are responding and giving your actual opinion of her.
